title: Kannada Alphabets (ಅಕ್ಷರಮಾಲೆ)
content: Kannada uses its own script (ಕನ್ನಡ ಲಿಪಿ). Learn vowels (ಸ್ವರ) and consonants (ವ್ಯಂಜನ) below.
intro: The Kannada script is an abugida of the Brahmic family. Below are the vowels and consonants with transliteration and usage examples.

title: Vowels and Consonants in Kannada and their pronunciation
content: Kannada script has 13 vowel letters and 34 consonant letters. Each consonant has an inherent vowel 'a' (ಅ).
intro: Kannada is written left-to-right. Short vowels are ಹ್ರಸ್ವ (hrasva), long vowels are ದೀರ್ಘ (dīrgha). Consonants are grouped by place of articulation: velar (ಕ-ಙ), palatal (ಚ-ಞ), retroflex (ಟ-ಣ), dental (ತ-ನ), labial (ಪ-ಮ), and unstructured (ಯ, ರ, ಲ, ವ, ಶ, ಷ, ಸ, ಹ, ಳ).

blocks.heading: Vowels (ಸ್ವರ)
blocks.content: ಅ (a) as in 'America', ಆ (ā) as in 'father', ಇ (i) as in 'bit', ಈ (ī) as in 'see', ಉ (u) as in 'put', ಊ (ū) as in 'food'. ಋ (ṛ) is used in Sanskrit words. ಎ (e) short e, ಏ (ē) long e, ಐ (ai) as in 'kite', ಒ (o) short o, ಓ (ō) long o, ಔ (au) as in 'how'. ಅಂ (aṃ) is anusvara (nasal), ಅಃ (aḥ) is visarga.

blocks.heading: Structured consonants
blocks.content: Velar: ಕ ಖ ಗ ಘ ಙ. Palatal: ಚ ಛ ಜ ಝ ಞ. Retroflex: ಟ ಠ ಡ ಢ ಣ. Dental: ತ ಥ ದ ಧ ನ. Labial: ಪ ಫ ಬ ಭ ಮ. Unstructured: ಯ ರ ಲ ವ ಶ ಷ ಸ ಹ ಳ.

title: Kannada Barakhadi : Vowel signs with consonants
content: Kannada Barakhadi: how vowels combine with consonants using diacritics.
intro: In Kannada, when a vowel follows a consonant, it is written with a diacritic (matra) rather than as a separate letter. The consonant without any diacritic has the inherent vowel ಅ (a). A consonant without a vowel is marked with halanta (್).

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content: Like in other Brahmic scripts, vowels are added to consonants using different symbols (diacritics). Each vowel has a sign that attaches to the consonant to form a syllable (ಅಕ್ಷರ akshara).

blocks.heading: ಅ (a) – Inherent, no change
blocks.content: Adding ಅ (a) has no visible change. ಕ (ka), ಬ (ba). Denoted as small 'a'.

blocks.heading: ಆ (ā) – Sign ಾ after consonant
blocks.content: ಕ + ಆ = ಕಾ (kā), ಬ + ಆ = ಬಾ (bā). Vertical stroke after the consonant.

blocks.heading: ಇ (i) – Sign ಿ before consonant
blocks.content: ಕ + ಇ = ಕಿ (ki), ಬ + ಇ = ಬಿ (bi). The sign appears before the consonant.

blocks.heading: ಈ (ī) – Sign ೀ after consonant
blocks.content: ಕ + ಈ = ಕೀ (kī), ಬ + ಈ = ಬೀ (bī). Two strokes after the consonant.

blocks.heading: ಉ (u) – Sign ು below
blocks.content: ಕ + ಉ = ಕು (ku), ಬ + ಉ = ಬು (bu). Curve below the consonant.

blocks.heading: ಊ (ū) – Sign ೂ below
blocks.content: ಕ + ಊ = ಕೂ (kū), ಬ + ಊ = ಬೂ (bū). Double curve below.

blocks.heading: ಎ (e) – Sign ೆ above
blocks.content: ಕ + ಎ = ಕೆ (ke), ಬ + ಎ = ಬೆ (be). Slanting line above.

blocks.heading: ಏ (ē) – Sign ೇ above
blocks.content: ಕ + ಏ = ಕೇ (kē), ಬ + ಏ = ಬೇ (bē). Two strokes above.

blocks.heading: ಐ (ai) – Sign ೈ
blocks.content: ಕ + ಐ = ಕೈ (kai), ಬ + ಐ = ಬೈ (bai). Used in words like ಕೈ (kai, hand).

blocks.heading: ಒ (o) – Sign ೊ
blocks.content: ಕ + ಒ = ಕೊ (ko), ಬ + ಒ = ಬೊ (bo). Hook and stroke.

blocks.heading: ಓ (ō) – Sign ೋ
blocks.content: ಕ + ಓ = ಕೋ (kō), ಬ + ಓ = ಬೋ (bō). Long o sound.

blocks.heading: ಔ (au) – Sign ೌ
blocks.content: ಕ + ಔ = ಕೌ (kau), ಬ + ಔ = ಬೌ (bau). As in ಕೌಶಿಕ (Kauśika).

blocks.heading: ಅಂ (aṃ) – Anusvara ಂ
blocks.content: ಕ + ಅಂ = ಕಂ (kaṃ). Dot above (anusvara) for nasalization.

blocks.heading: ಅಃ (aḥ) – Visarga ಃ
blocks.content: ಕ + ಅಃ = ಕಃ (kaḥ). Two dots after for visarga (rare in modern Kannada).

blocks.heading: Half consonant (halanta ್)
blocks.content: To write a consonant without the inherent vowel, add the halanta (್) below: ಕ್ (k), ಬ್ (b). The resulting form is ಅರ್ಧಾಕ್ಷರ (ardhākshara).

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Kannada
content: Tips to differentiate similar-sounding consonants and vowels in Kannada.
intro: Learners often confuse similar-sounding pairs. Listening to Kannada and practising pairs (ತ-ಟ, ದ-ಡ, ನ-ಣ, ಲ-ಳ, ಶ-ಷ-ಸ) helps. Use the speaker buttons to hear each pair.

blocks.heading: Dental vs Retroflex
blocks.content: ತ (ta) is dental, ಟ (ṭa) is retroflex (tongue curled back). ದ (da) is dental, ಡ (ḍa) is retroflex. ನ (na) is dental, ಣ (ṇa) is retroflex. Practice: ತಾರೆ (star) vs ಟೋಪಿ (cap); ದಾರಿ (path) vs ಡಬ್ಬಿ (box).

blocks.heading: Sibilants ಶ ಷ ಸ
blocks.content: ಶ (śa) – palatal, as in ಶಾಂತ (peace). ಷ (ṣa) – retroflex, used mainly in Sanskrit words. ಸ (sa) – dental, as in ಸಾಗರ (sea). In modern Kannada, ಶ and ಷ are often pronounced similarly.

blocks.heading: ಲ (la) vs ಳ (ḷa)
blocks.content: ಲ is dental lateral; ಳ is retroflex lateral (tip of tongue curled). Example: ಕಾಲ (time/foot) vs ಕಾಳ (black).

title: Pronunciation of Anusvara (ಅಂ) and Visarga (ಅಃ)
content: How to pronounce anusvara (ಂ) and visarga (ಃ) in Kannada.
intro: Anusvara (ಂ) is a nasal sign. Visarga (ಃ) is a breath sound, mostly in Sanskrit loanwords.

blocks.heading: Anusvara (ಅಂ)
blocks.content: The anusvara (ಂ) is written as a dot above the consonant. It represents nasalization. Before velar consonants (ಕ, ಖ, ಗ, ಘ, ಙ) it sounds like ṅ; before palatals like ñ; before retroflex/dental like ṇ or n; before labials like m. Example: ಅಂಗ (aṅga, body), ಕಂಬಳಿ (kambali, blanket).

blocks.heading: Visarga (ಅಃ)
blocks.content: Visarga (ಃ) is two dots after a vowel or consonant. It indicates a slight 'h' or breath release. Common in Sanskrit words: ದುಃಖ (sorrow), ಅಃ (ah). In everyday Kannada it is less frequent.

title: Combining consonants in Kannada (ಒತ್ತಕ್ಷರ)
content: Combining consonants in Kannada – ಒತ್ತಕ್ಷರ (ottakshara).
intro: When two or more consonants appear together without a vowel between them, the first consonant is written in a half (subjoined) form. Half form + full consonant = ಒತ್ತಕ್ಷರ (conjunct).

blocks.heading: Introduction
blocks.content: In English, consonants are written one after another (e.g. 'fact', 'plant'). In Kannada, the first consonant is written in a reduced form (halanta + shape) below or attached to the next consonant. For example: ಕ + ಕ = ಕ್ಕ (kka), ಕ + ನ = ಕ್ನ (kna).

blocks.heading: Halanta (್)
blocks.content: The halanta (್) removes the inherent 'a' from a consonant. So ಕ್ is just 'k'. When another consonant follows, the conjunct is formed: ಕ್ + ನ = ಕ್ನ (kna), as in ವಿದ್ಯಾರ್ಥಿ (student).

blocks.heading: Common conjuncts
blocks.content: ಕ್ರ (kra), ಗ್ರ (gra), ತ್ರ (tra), ದ್ರ (dra), ಪ್ರ (pra), ಕ್ಷ (kṣa). Example words: ಕರ್ಣಾಟಕ (Karnataka), ವಿದ್ಯಾರ್ಥಿ (student), ಕ್ಷಣ (moment).

title: Combining consonants with ರ (r) - Part 2
content: How ರ (r) combines with other consonants in Kannada.
intro: ರ (r) has special conjunct forms. When ರ comes after another consonant (e.g. /pra/, /kra/), it is written as a sign (arkāvottu) above or attached to the preceding consonant. When ರ comes first (half r), the following consonant gets a curve or sign.

blocks.heading: ರ as second consonant
blocks.content: ಕ + ರ = ಕ್ರ (kra), ಗ + ರ = ಗ್ರ (gra), ಪ + ರ = ಪ್ರ (pra), ತ + ರ = ತ್ರ (tra). Example: ಪ್ರೇಮ (love), ಕ್ರಮ (order). The ರ is written as a curved stroke (ರ್ಕಾವೊತ್ತು) attached to the first consonant.

blocks.heading: ರ as first consonant
blocks.content: When ರ is the first (half) consonant: ರ + ಕ = ರ್ಕ. In a word like ಕರ್ನಾಟಕ (Karnataka), the sequence /rna/ is written ರ್ನಾ – the conjunct form of ರ appears before ನ.

title: Combining consonants with ಹ (h) - Part 3
content: Combining ಹ (h) with other consonants in Kannada.
intro: ಹ (ha) is less commonly used in conjunct form than ರ. When ಹ is the first consonant (half), it may take a subjoined form in words borrowed from Sanskrit.

blocks.heading: ಹ in conjuncts
blocks.content: Examples: ಹ + ನ = ಹ್ನ (hna), as in ಪ್ರಹ್ನ (Prahna). In modern Kannada, many such conjuncts are simplified or the word is written with halanta: ಹ್ + ನ = ಹ್ನ. Common words: ದುಃಖ (sorrow) uses visarga, not ಹ conjunct.

title: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants
content: Special conjuncts and ligatures in Kannada script.
intro: Some consonant combinations have special or traditional ligature forms. ಕ್ಷ (kṣa), ಜ್ಞ (jña), and ರ-conjuncts are among the most common.

blocks.heading: ಕ್ಷ (kṣa)
blocks.content: ಕ + ಷ = ಕ್ಷ. Used in ಕ್ಷಣ (moment), ಕ್ಷೇತ್ರ (field), ವಿದ್ಯಾಕ್ಷರ (letter). Very common in Sanskrit-origin words.

blocks.heading: ಜ್ಞ (jña)
blocks.content: ಜ + ಞ = ಜ್ಞ. Used in ಜ್ಞಾನ (knowledge), ವಿಜ್ಞಾನ (science).

blocks.heading: Nasal + consonant
blocks.content: Nasals (ಙ ಞ ಣ ನ ಮ) are often written as anusvara (ಂ) when preceding another consonant: ಬೆಂಗಳೂರು (Bengaluru) uses ಂ before ಗ.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Kannada
content: Pronouns and Articles in Kannada
intro: Learn Kannada pronouns. Use the speaker buttons to hear pronunciation. Kannada has no articles like 'a', 'an', 'the'.

title: Using plurals to indicate respect (ಆದರಾರ್ಥೀ ಬಹುವಚನ)
content: Using plurals to indicate respect in Kannada.
intro: In Kannada, using plural forms for 'you' (ನೀವು) and 'they' (ಅವರು) shows respect. Singular ನೀನು is informal.

title: Verbs in Kannada
content: Common verbs in Kannada with infinitive and usage.
intro: Kannada verb stems combine with tense suffixes. Below are common verbs in infinitive form.

title: Simple Present Tense in Kannada
content: Simple Present Tense in Kannada
intro: Formula: Subject + verb stem + present suffix. Suffix depends on person and gender: -ತ್ತೇನೆ (I), -ತ್ತೀಯ (you sg), -ತ್ತಾನೆ (he), -ತ್ತಾಳೆ (she), -ತ್ತೇವೆ (we), -ತ್ತೀರಿ (you pl), -ತ್ತಾರೆ (they).

blocks.heading: Present tense suffixes (verb stem + utta + suffix)

title: Simple Present Tense of "To Be" in Kannada
content: "To Be" in Kannada is ಇರು (iru). Different forms for each pronoun.
intro: In Kannada "To Be" is ಇರು (iru). Present forms: ನಾನು ಇದ್ದೇನೆ (I am), ನೀನು ಇದ್ದೀಯ (you are), ಅವನು ಇದ್ದಾನೆ (he is), ಅವಳು ಇದ್ದಾಳೆ (she is), ನಾವು ಇದ್ದೇವೆ (we are), ಅವರು ಇದ್ದಾರೆ (they are).

title: Present Continuous Tense in Kannada
content: Present Continuous in Kannada – action happening now.
intro: Form: subject + verb stem + -ತ್ತ + ಇರು in present. E.g. ನಾನು ಮಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದೇನೆ (I am doing).

title: Simple Future Tense in Kannada
content: Simple Future Tense in Kannada.
intro: Future is formed with verb stem + -ುವ/-ಉವ + personal suffix. E.g. ನಾನು ಮಾಡುವೆನೆ (I will do), ಅವನು ಬರುವಾನೆ (He will come).

title: Future Continuous Tense in Kannada
content: Future Continuous in Kannada.
intro: Action that will be ongoing at a future time. Form: future of main verb + ಇರು.

title: Simple Past Tense in Kannada – Part 1 (Verbs without object)
content: Simple Past Tense – intransitive verbs.
intro: Past tense: verb stem changes + past suffix. ನಾನು ಹೋದೆ (I went), ಅವನು ಬಂದ (He came).

title: Simple Past Tense in Kannada – Part 2 (Verbs with object)
content: Simple Past Tense – transitive verbs.
intro: With object, same past stem; subject suffix agrees with subject. ನಾನು ಅನ್ನ ತಿಂದೆ (I ate food).

title: Simple Past Tense in Kannada – Part 3 (Past tense of "to be")
content: Past tense of "To Be" (ಇರು) in Kannada.
intro: ನಾನು ಇದ್ದೆ (I was), ಅವನು ಇದ್ದ (He was), ಅವರು ಇದ್ದರು (They were).

title: Exceptional Verbs in Kannada which change in past tense form
content: Irregular past tense verbs in Kannada.
intro: Some verbs have irregular past stems. E.g. ಬರು (come) → ಬಂದ (came), ತಿನ್ನು (eat) → ತಿಂದ (ate).

title: Past Continuous Tense in Kannada
content: Past Continuous in Kannada.
intro: Action that was ongoing in the past. Verb + -ತ್ತ + ಇರು in past.

title: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Kannada
content: Perfect tenses in Kannada.
intro: Perfect: completed action. Form uses past participle + ಇರು in present/past/future.

title: Learn Prepositions in Kannada
content: Common prepositions and postpositions in Kannada.
intro: Kannada uses postpositions (after the noun). Common ones: -ಅಲ್ಲಿ (at), -ಕ್ಕೆ/ಇಗೆ (to), -ದಿಂದ (from), -ಜೊತೆ (with).

title: Preposition "TO" in Kannada
content: How to say "to" (direction/goal) in Kannada.
intro: "To" is usually -ಕ್ಕೆ (kke) or -ಇಗೆ (ige) added to the noun. ಮನೆ + ಕ್ಕೆ = ಮನೆಕ್ಕೆ (to home).

title: Sentences with person or living things as object
content: Using dative case for person as object in Kannada.
intro: When the object is a person, use dative -ಕ್ಕೆ/ಇಗೆ or -ಅನು/ಅನ್ನು. ನನಗೆ ಕೊಡು (Give to me).

title: Saying My/His/Her in Kannada
content: Possessive forms (my, your, his, her) in Kannada.
intro: Possessives: ನನ್ನ (my), ನಿಮ್ಮ (your), ಅವನ (his), ಅವಳ (her), ನಮ್ಮ (our), ಅವರ (their).

title: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Kannada
content: Forming yes/no and WH questions in Kannada.
intro: Add -ಅ (a) or -ಏ (ē) for yes/no. WH words: ಏನು (what), ಯಾರು (who), ಎಲ್ಲಿ (where), ಯಾವಾಗ (when), ಏಕೆ (why), ಹೇಗೆ (how).

title: Negative Sentences – Present tense in Kannada
content: Negative present tense in Kannada.
intro: Use ಇಲ್ಲ (illa) for "is not" / "do not". ನಾನು ಮಾಡುವದಿಲ್ಲ (I do not do).

title: Negative Sentences – Past tense in Kannada
content: Negative past tense in Kannada.
intro: Past negative: ಮಾಡಲಿಲ್ಲ (did not do), ಬರಲಿಲ್ಲ (did not come).

title: Negative Sentence – Future Tense in Kannada
content: Negative future in Kannada.
intro: Future negative: ಮಾಡುವದಿಲ್ಲ or ಮಾಡೆನು (will not do).

title: Working with nouns – Gender & Plurals in Kannada
content: Gender and plural formation in Kannada nouns.
intro: Kannada has three genders: masculine, feminine, neuter. Plurals often add -ಗಳು (gaḷu) or -ರು (ru) for humans.

title: Working with nouns – Prepositions in Kannada
content: Nouns with postpositions in Kannada.
intro: Nouns take case suffixes: -ಅಲ್ಲಿ, -ಕ್ಕೆ, -ದಿಂದ, etc.

title: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings
content: Prepositions that sound similar or have close meanings.
intro: ಕ್ಕೆ/ಇಗೆ (to), ಅಲ್ಲಿ/ಇಲ್ಲಿ (at), ದಿಂದ/ಇಂದ (from) – choose based on vowel harmony and context.

title: Cases in Kannada. ವಿಭಕ್ತಿ Vibhakti and ವಿಭಕ್ತಿಪ್ರತ್ಯಯ
content: Grammatical cases (vibhakti) in Kannada.
intro: Kannada has seven cases: nominative, accusative, instrumental, dative, ablative, genitive, locative. Suffixes attach to the noun stem.

title: Commands / Imperative statements in Kannada
content: Imperative (commands) in Kannada.
intro: Imperative: use verb stem or stem + -ಅಲಿ/-ಅಿ. ನೀನು: ಮಾಡು (do!), ನೀವು: ಮಾಡಿ (do! respectful).

title: Time related words
content: Time vocabulary in Kannada.
intro: Words for time, day, hour, yesterday, tomorrow, etc.

title: Verbs indicating cause of action. Causative verb
content: Causative verbs in Kannada.
intro: Causative: make someone do something. Often -ಿಸು (-isu) suffix: ಕೇಳು (hear) → ಕೇಳಿಸು (make hear / ask).

title: ಆ-ಇ-ಎ-ಎ-ಯಾ-ಈ (A-I-e-e-yA-I) rule
content: Adjective agreement rule in Kannada (like Marathi आ-ई-ए-ए-या-ई).
intro: Adjectives and some modifiers change ending by gender/number: masculine singular -ಆ, feminine -ಇ/ಈ, neuter -ಎ, plural -ಅರು/ಗಳು etc.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style
content: Alternative perfect tense formation in Kannada.
intro: Perfect can also be expressed with past participle + ಇರು in different tenses.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Kannada
content: Perfect continuous tenses in Kannada.
intro: Action that had been / has been / will have been ongoing. Verb + -ತ್ತ + ಇರು in perfect form.

title: Difference between ಇಲ್ಲ (illa) and ಬೇಡ (bēḍa)
content: When to use ಇಲ್ಲ (no/not) vs ಬೇಡ (don't want).
intro: ಇಲ್ಲ (illa) = is not / do not / no. ಬೇಡ (bēḍa) = don't want / no thanks.

title: Sentence using "Going to" phrase
content: "Going to" for future intention in Kannada.
intro: Use ಹೋಗು (go) + infinitive or -ಲು/-ಕ್ಕೆ form to express 'going to do'.

blocks.heading: Going to – examples
rows: We are going to the market
She is going to write a letter
They are going to watch a movie
I am going to Bangalore tomorrow

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase
content: "Used to" (past habit) in Kannada.
intro: Past habit: verb stem + -ತ್ತಿದ್ದ + past suffix. ಅವನು ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಬರುತ್ತಿದ್ದ (He used to come here).

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style
content: Alternative way to say "used to" in Kannada.
intro: Same idea with different phrasing: ನಾನು ಹೋಗುತ್ತಿದ್ದೆ (I used to go).

title: Sentence using "If-Then"
content: If-then conditional sentences in Kannada.
intro: ಒಂದು ವೇಳೆ ... ಅಂದರೆ (if ... then). Or -ಅಲ್ಲಿ/-ಅಾದರೆ on verb.

blocks.heading: If-Then examples
rows: If you come, I will go
If it rains, we will stay
If you study, you will pass
If there is time, we will meet

title: Prepositions with Verbs
content: Verbs that take specific postpositions in Kannada.
intro: Some verbs require -ಅನ್ನು, -ಇಗೆ, -ದಿಂದ etc.

title: Adjectives in Kannada
content: Adjectives and their agreement in Kannada.
intro: Adjectives may agree in gender/number. ಚಿಕ್ಕ (small), ಚಿಕ್ಕದು (small thing), ಚಿಕ್ಕವನು (small boy).

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to"
content: Expressing ability (can / be able to) in Kannada.
intro: Use -ಲು ಸಾಧ್ಯ (can), or -ಬಲ್ಲ (able): ನಾನು ಮಾಡಬಲ್ಲೆ (I can do).

title: Using verb To Want/To Need
content: To want and to need in Kannada.
intro: ಬೇಕು (want/need), ಬಯಸು (want). ನನಗೆ ಬೇಕು (I want/need).

title: Verb "To Become/To Happen"
content: To become / to happen in Kannada.
intro: ಆಗು (āgu) = become, happen. ಅವನು ಶಿಕ್ಷಕನಾಗಿದ್ದಾನೆ (He became a teacher).

title: Using "Should"
content: Expressing "should" (obligation/advice) in Kannada.
intro: ಬೇಕು or -ಬೇಕು with verb. ನೀವು ಹೋಗಬೇಕು (You should go).

title: Using "Must"/"to be have to"
content: Must / have to in Kannada.
intro: ಬೇಕು (must/have to). ನಾನು ಮಾಡಬೇಕು (I must do).

title: Using phrase "to keep doing"
content: To keep doing (continuous repetition) in Kannada.
intro: Verb + -ತ್ತಲೇ ಇರು or -ಉತ್ತಿರು. ಅವನು ಮಾಡುತ್ತಲೇ ಇದ್ದಾನೆ (He keeps doing).

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison
content: Comparatives and superlatives in Kannada.
intro: More: -ಇಂದ (than). Most: ಅತ್ಯಂತ or -ಅವಳಲ್ಲಿ. ದೊಡ್ಡದು (big), ಅದಕ್ಕಿಂತ ದೊಡ್ಡದು (bigger).

title: Using verb "To Know"
content: To know in Kannada.
